task_completion.description: Verify that the FreeCAD application is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window and user interface are visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the cylindrical shank.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D cylinder is visible in the FreeCAD viewport, or a corresponding feature (e.g., Cylinder, Pad) is present in the Tree View.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the rectangular fork head block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular 3D block is visible attached to one end of the cylinder in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Verify that a slot is cut into the fork head.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangular block has a central gap or slot removed from it, creating a U-shaped, two-pronged fork.

task_completion.description: Verify that a through-hole is created in the fork head.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circular hole is visible passing completely through both prongs of the fork head.

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar, the root of the Tree View, or a save dialog explicitly displays 'clevis_rod_end.FCStd'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the FreeCAD graphical interface to build the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with FreeCAD menus, toolbars, workbenches (e.g., Part or PartDesign), and the 3D viewport, rather than just executing a script in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the model was built step-by-step rather than loading a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows the model in various stages of completion (e.g., 2D sketches, partial 3D extrusions, subsequent cuts) instead of the finished model appearing instantly.

integrity.description: Verify the result is a genuine 3D model inside FreeCAD.
integrity.visual_evidence: The object is rendered in the FreeCAD 3D viewport with standard UI elements like the view cube, coordinate cross, and a populated Tree View showing the construction history.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'clevis_rod_end.FCStd'. The final model's bounding box must span from [-20, -15, -15] to [20, 15, 15]. The through-hole diameter is exactly 10mm, and the slot width is exactly 12mm. A volume tolerance of 10% is acceptable.
